steps: Open the Leave Tracker from the left sidebar
Click on 'Apply Leave'
Choose the appropriate leave type (Sick, Casual, Earned, etc.)
Select the dates (full day or half day)
Add a reason for your leave
Click on Submit to send your request
rules: Your leave request will need approval from your manager
Make sure you select the correct leave type
Check your leave balance before applying
Apply at least 1 day in advance for planned leaves
mistakes: Applying without checking available leave balance
faq: Q: How long does approval take? A: Usually within 24 hours, depends on your manager.
Q: Can I cancel a leave after applying? A: Yes, go to Leave Tracker and cancel the pending request.
Q: What if I don't have enough balance? A: You may apply for Leave Without Pay (LWP) — check with HR.

steps: Open Leave Tracker
Click on 'Apply Leave'
Select the leave type
Choose the date
Select 'Half Day' option (First Half or Second Half)
Enter reason and submit
rules: Half day option must be selected correctly — First Half or Second Half
Manager approval is required
Half day counts as 0.5 from your leave balance
faq: Q: Does half day deduct full leave? A: No, it deducts only 0.5 from your balance.

steps: Go to Leave Tracker and check the rejection reason your manager mentioned
Make corrections if needed (wrong dates, wrong type, missing reason)
Reapply with proper details
If still unclear, talk to your manager directly for clarification
rules: Manager decision is final unless discussed
Always provide a proper reason while applying
Team availability can also be a reason for rejection during busy periods
faq: Q: Can I appeal a rejected leave? A: Yes, speak to your manager or HR directly.

keywords: how many leaves do i have
steps: Open Zoho People
Go to Leave Tracker from the left sidebar
Click on 'Leave Balance' or 'My Leaves'
You will see a breakdown of all leave types and remaining balance
rules: Leave balance resets at the start of the financial year
Earned leaves may carry forward — check with HR for policy
faq: Q: When do leaves reset? A: At the start of the financial year.
Q: Do unused leaves carry forward? A: Earned leaves may, casual leaves typically don't — confirm with HR.

steps: Go to Time Tracker and click on 'Log Time'
Select the correct client, project, and job
Add a short description of the work you did
Enter your working hours
Click on Save and then submit your timesheet
rules: Timesheets should be submitted every week — usually by Friday EOD
All fields (client, project, job, description) are required
Do not leave timesheets pending for more than a week
faq: Q: What if I forget to submit on Friday? A: Submit ASAP and inform your manager.
Q: Can I edit a submitted timesheet? A: Only before it's approved — contact your manager if already approved.

steps: Open the Zoho People mobile app
Tap on 'Check-in' when you start your shift
Tap on 'Check-out' once your shift ends
rules: Must be done daily
Follow your assigned working hours
Check-in and check-out both are mandatory
mistakes: Forgetting check-out — this will show as incomplete attendance
faq: Q: What if I forget to check out? A: Apply for attendance regularization immediately.
Q: Can I check in from home? A: Depends on your company's WFH policy — check with HR.

when_to_use: Forgot to check in or out
steps: Go to Attendance section in Zoho People
Click on 'Regularization' or 'Attendance Regularization'
Select the date for which you need to fix attendance
Enter the correct check-in and check-out timings
Add a reason (e.g. forgot to check in, app not working)
Submit the request and wait for manager approval
rules: Manager approval is required
Must be submitted as soon as possible — don't delay
Repeated regularization requests may be flagged by HR
faq: Q: How many times can I regularize? A: There's usually a monthly limit — check with HR.
Q: What if my manager doesn't approve? A: Follow up with your manager or raise it with HR.

steps: Open Zoho People
Go to the 'Payroll' or 'My Pay' section from the sidebar
Select the month for which you need the payslip
Click Download or View Payslip
rules: Payslips are available after salary is processed (usually after 1st of each month)
Contact HR if payslip is not visible
faq: Q: When is the payslip available? A: Usually after the 1st or 5th of each month after salary processing.
Q: What if payslip shows wrong amount? A: Contact HR immediately.

steps: Open GitHub Desktop
Click 'File' → 'Clone Repository'
Select repo from the list or paste the URL
Choose a local folder to save it
Click 'Clone' and wait for it to finish
rules: You must have repository access before cloning
Login with your company GitHub account — not personal
Ask your manager or team lead for access if you don't have it
mistakes: Not having repo access before trying to clone
Using personal GitHub account instead of company account
faq: Q: Where should I clone the repo? A: Usually in a dedicated 'Projects' folder on your system.
Q: What if clone fails? A: Check your GitHub login and make sure you have access to that repo.

steps: Check if you're logged into the correct company GitHub account
Verify that you actually have access to that repository
Request access from your manager or the repository owner
Once access is granted, try cloning again
rules: Access must be granted before cloning
Always use company GitHub account — not personal
faq: Q: Who do I ask for repo access? A: Your manager or the team lead who owns the repository.
Q: How long does access take? A: Usually within a few hours once requested.

steps: Make sure you're on the correct base branch (usually 'main' or 'develop')
Pull latest changes first
Click 'Current Branch' at the top
Select 'New Branch'
Enter a branch name following your team's naming convention
Click 'Create Branch'
rules: Always pull latest code before creating a branch
Follow your team's naming convention (e.g. feature/login-page, fix/header-bug)
Never work directly on main or master branch
faq: Q: What should I name my branch? A: Follow team convention like feature/your-feature-name or fix/bug-name.
Q: Which base branch to use? A: Ask your team lead — usually 'main' or 'develop'.

steps: Open GitHub Desktop
Make sure you're on the right branch
Click 'Fetch origin' to check for new changes
Click 'Pull origin' to download them
rules: Always pull before starting any new work
Pull before pushing your own changes to avoid conflicts
faq: Q: What's the difference between fetch and pull? A: Fetch checks for changes, pull actually downloads them.
Q: What if I get a conflict after pulling? A: Resolve conflicts in your code editor, then commit.

steps: Make your code changes
In GitHub Desktop, you'll see your changed files listed
Write a short commit message describing what you changed
Click 'Commit to [branch name]'
Click 'Push origin' to upload your changes to GitHub
rules: Always write a meaningful commit message — not just 'changes' or 'fix'
Commit small, logical chunks of work — not everything at once
Never push directly to main — use a branch
faq: Q: How often should I commit? A: After each logical chunk of work — don't wait too long.
Q: What's a good commit message? A: Something like 'Add login validation' or 'Fix header alignment bug'.

steps: Switch to the branch you want to merge INTO (e.g. main)
Click 'Branch' in the top menu
Click 'Merge into current branch'
Select the branch you want to merge
Resolve any conflicts if they appear
Commit the merge
rules: Always test your code before merging
Get code review/approval before merging into main
Resolve all conflicts before completing the merge
faq: Q: What if there are merge conflicts? A: Open the conflicting files in your editor, resolve them, then commit.
Q: Can I merge without review? A: Depends on team policy — usually no for main branch.

steps: Push your branch to GitHub first
In GitHub Desktop, click 'Create Pull Request' — it opens GitHub in browser
Add a clear title describing your changes
Add a description of what you did and why
Assign reviewers (usually your team lead)
Submit the PR
rules: Code review is required before merging
Add a clear description — don't leave it blank
Tag the right reviewers, not random people
faq: Q: Who should I add as reviewer? A: Your team lead or whoever owns the codebase.
Q: Can I push more commits after creating a PR? A: Yes, they automatically update the PR.

steps: GitHub Desktop will highlight files with conflicts
Open the conflicting file in your code editor
Look for conflict markers: <<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>
Keep the correct version of the code (yours, theirs, or a combination)
Delete the conflict markers
Save the file, then go back to GitHub Desktop
Commit the resolved conflict
rules: Never panic — conflicts are normal and part of team development
When unsure which code to keep, ask your team lead
Always test after resolving a conflict
faq: Q: How do I avoid conflicts? A: Pull the latest code frequently before working.
Q: What do the conflict markers mean? A: <<<<<<< is your code, >>>>>>> is incoming code, ======= separates them.
